prompt:
Ticket from the analytics-tooling squad. Analysts keep pasting SQL-ish strings into our report builder and today we regex-mangle them. I want a real front end: parse a tight SQL subset and emit a typed logical plan we can hand to the (separate, existing) executor. Parsing and planning ONLY — nothing in this ticket runs a query. TypeScript on node (node --test, type-stripping: plain annotations, no enums), zero dependencies, hand-rolled lexer/parser — no parser generators. Put the public surface in sqlplan.ts (exporting parse, plan, SqlError); split lexer/parser/planner into their own modules behind it if you like.

Grammar (exactly this, nothing more): SELECT (* | col [, col]...) FROM table [WHERE pred [AND pred]...] [ORDER BY col [ASC|DESC] [, col [ASC|DESC]]...] [LIMIT n [OFFSET m]]. A pred is col OP literal with OP one of = != < <= > >=, or col IN (literal [, literal]...) — parenthesised and non-empty. Literals: single-quoted strings ('' escapes a quote), numbers (optional leading minus, optional decimal part), true, false, null. Keywords are case-insensitive and reserved; identifiers ([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*) keep their case. Clause order is fixed; anything after a complete query is an error.

parse(sql) returns exactly { columns: '*' | string[], table, where, orderBy, limit, offset }. where is null or an expression tree: AND nodes are { op: 'and', left, right } and associate LEFT; comparisons are { op, column, value }; IN is { op: 'in', column, values }. orderBy is [] or [{ column, dir }] with dir 'asc' (the default) or 'desc'. limit and offset are null when their clause is absent — offset stays null when only LIMIT is given. LIMIT/OFFSET arguments must be non-negative integers.

plan(sql, catalog) — catalog is { [table]: { columns: string[] } } — parses and returns a plan tree built bottom-up: { node: 'scan', table }, wrapped by { node: 'filter', input, predicates } ONLY when there is a WHERE (predicates = the AND chain flattened, source order), wrapped by { node: 'sort', input, keys } only when there is an ORDER BY, wrapped by { node: 'slice', input, offset, limit } only when LIMIT and/or OFFSET appear (offset defaults to 0 there), and always topped by { node: 'project', input, columns } where '*' expands to the catalog's column order. Absent clauses produce NO node. The planner validates names: unknown table "x" / unknown column "c" in table "t" (SqlError, exact phrasing). Neither function may mutate the catalog; both are pure.

Errors are the other half of the contract. SqlError extends Error; parse errors carry a 1-based col property and messages of exactly the form col N: expected X, found "lexeme" — the lexeme is the raw source text of the offending token, end of input is the phrase (unquoted) for exhaustion with N = input length + 1. Expectations used: SELECT, column name or *, FROM, table name, column name, a comparison operator or IN, a value, (, ) or comma, BY, a non-negative integer, end of input. Lexer failures: col N: unexpected character "~" and col N: unterminated string (N = the opening quote's column). A negative or fractional LIMIT/OFFSET is reported as expected a non-negative integer with the number's raw lexeme.

test_sqlplan.ts is the acceptance contract — don't touch it; node --test test_sqlplan.ts must pass.
